description: I had a better prepared performance with faster and more accurate delivery results
author: Anthony R.
pros: It is a very modern platform that through graphics allows to understand each data in a more understandable and effective way and even more so when data is unified that shows the most important and is easier to understand
cons: the platform is complex to learn to use, its learning time is long and a bit confusing at first

review_title: Stable, performant, well-featured, well-documented, has a great ecosystem
description: Very positive. It's stable, performant, well-featured, well-documented, has a great ecosystem. The dev team is responsive to feature requests & fixes.
author: Conrad L.
pros: Very fully featured, and constantly evolving. Produced by a knowledgeable and professional team.
cons: Enterprise license is expensive; if you don't use all the Stardog-specific features then Amazon's hosted Neptune service might be better value.

author: Aarno A.
pros: Stardog fulfilled all requirements for VSHN to build a Triplestore-as-a-Service offering
cons: Service provider requirements differ from regular enterprise customers

description: Combining of standard semantic graph capabilities with well selected features outside of standard creates perfect environment for the delivering of future looking artificial intelligence applications in the enterprise environment.
author: Vladimir M.
pros: Integration of analytical capabilities with the graph store, visual studio for the developer productivity, virtualization of data and data integration capabilities
cons: Need a cloud product offer, which I hope coming soon

description: navigate through the data to have a better interpretation of them and learn to analyze them from another perspective
author: Thomas C.
pros: It has been a platform with a powerful data integration structure with easy installation, the database can be viewed in a more analytical way, improving the knowledge that can be had about this data.
cons: the learning curve is a bit complex because it is not a basic knowledge platform, you must have extensive knowledge of this type of platform to know how to use it correctly

review_title: Stardog, the intelligent NoSQL Database, with a power schema, and a great integration platform
description: At 3 different clients, I have participated in the creation of different solutions as: (1) CMDB application including management of configuration items and impact analysis in case of failure. (2) Integration of external market data with internal sources, including Master Data Management of Organizations. (3) Real estate transactions analysis using spatial functions (4) Human resource profiles access using full text search (5) Human resource competency management (6) Branch search using spatial functions
author: Daniel L.
pros: As a RDF NoSQL database, Stardog is the best of both worlds: flexible as a NoSQL DB but with a powerful schema. Also, the product is a complete integration platform as it enables, not only virtually accessing other SQL and NoSQL databases, but also the integration of unstructured information. The fact that text search and spatial functions are also provided completes Stardog's abilities as a full integration platform.
cons: As a newer DBMS, Stardog will still need investment in the areas of database management and monitoring to make it easier to deploy, maintain and support.

description: Support is a place where Stardog shines. It felt more like a partner relationship rather than a client / vendor one. For example, when we required the PATH operator and GRAPHQL support which Stardog did not support at the time, we work with them to defined and/or test the features which were release in months of requesting it.
author: Serge C.
pros: Stardog «PATH» operator allows us to do path traversal queries which would have been very difficult otherwise. A close second are the virtual graph capabilities and the simplification of R2RML via SMS2.
cons: Stardog is easy to deploy for simple installation, but cluster management and monitoring is rudimentary at best.
